mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0% signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending power, which forces S&P 500 earnings downgrades and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or signal loses predictive power with p-value above 0.1 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital goods demand recovery, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive returns with Rank IC below 0.01, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production below zero percent reflects immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term equity sell-offs within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth does not correspond to negative 5-day returns with win rate below 45%, or signal reverses direction during documented supply chain shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 225.0 index threshold forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in greater than 58% of periods above threshold, or inflation threshold loses predictive accuracy across rolling 2-year validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth correlates with negative forward returns with Rank IC below negative 0.02, or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over 3-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to broader output, which signals impending earnings revisions and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Divergence metric fails to predict negative returns with win rate below 40%, or interaction effect vanishes when controlling for concurrent monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surpassing the 95.0 index level confirms robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain stability, which reduces operational risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of instances above threshold, or signal loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample validation.
